latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: fox night: they're not just cute
latest.script_key: watch the fox move those ears aren t just cute you ll notice his head tilts 180 degrees scanning for mice rustling in the grass that s how he locks onto the sound then pounces in total silence no squealing no warning because foxes hunt by sound not scent would you trust your ears like that

latest.title: Woodpeckers don't get headaches from drilling
latest.script_key: watch this one twice that beak hits the tree at 16 mph that s why it doesn t crack the skull s built like a shock absorber tight bone fluid cushion even a special eyelid that slides shut mid peck so nothing jiggles loose i love this one

latest.title: Monkeys in cities don't act like zoo monkeys
latest.script_key: they re not pets watch this guy s left hand he s not holding anything he s using it like a fifth foot to balance while he reaches city monkeys figure out where the food is not where the keepers are that s why they re bolder than zoo monkeys what would you do if one walked toward you

latest.title: forests breathe through living roots
latest.script_key: these trees aren t just standing there watch the bark rise and fall like lungs because their roots pump air underground feeding fungi and each other that s why i never knew trees breathed like this did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Foxes scavenge trash cans at night
latest.script_key: trash can raider at night but it s not stealing snacks that fridge light it lures moths right to the door that s why foxes wait in the dark then pounce watch your bins tonight you might be the main course

latest.title: Tigers Los tigres usan rayas para camuflarse mejor
latest.script_key: los tigres rompen su silueta con rayas f jate en c mo las rayas desarman su contorno entre pasto y sombra dividiendo su forma en pedazos que el cerebro no encaja f cil esa es su trampa visual no es decoraci n es supervivencia t los ver as antes now the stripe stealth at the start makes sense

latest.title: Elephants paint themselves with mud
latest.script_key: elephants are artists too watch those gray patches shift color those aren t paint they re mud they fling it with trunks like sunscreen because elephant skin s so sensitive sunlight burns that s why they slather on the mud before the heat hits ever seen one skip this step they look like they re wearing mismatched socks

latest.title: Foxes see city streetlights differently
latest.script_key: watch the fox s eyes that flicker in the pupils that s tapetum lucidum reflecting every streetlight because foxes don t need to see colors at night they see movement and light that s why this one spots the car before it even slows down did you think those lights just looked cool

latest.title: Coral reefs are cities built by tiny animals
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: fox night: why car headlights help foxes hunt
latest.script_key: headlights are fox bait that red fox didn t come for the car it came for the mice fleeing the light mice freeze when bright beams hit that s why that s why the fox stalks slow sure you d freeze too if a spotlight pinned you

latest.title: trees don't bend they brace against wind
latest.script_key: storms like this don t break trees here s what blew my mind that trunk isn t swaying it s locking the roots pivot like anchors until the wind cuts through the canopy that s why the tree stays put while the branches whip around that sway you see isn t weakness it s a survival dance
